title: The Geography of Immersed Lagrangian Fillings of Legendrian Submanifolds
speaker: Lisa Traynor
abstract: Given a smooth knot K in the 3-sphere, a classic question in knot theory is: What surfaces in the 4-ball have boundary equal to K? One can also consider immersed surfaces and ask a “geography” question: What combinations of genus and double points can be realized by surfaces with boundary equal to K? I will discuss symplectic analogues of these questions: Given a Legendrian knot, what Lagrangian surfaces can it bound? What immersed Lagrangian surfaces can it bound?

proof_stages.label: Smooth geography baseline
proof_stages.summary: The talk first defines the geography problem for smooth knot fillings by plotting genus or Betti data against transverse double points, records the elementary north/east/southeast moves, and uses four-ball genus and four-ball crossing number as comparison data.

proof_stages.label: Lagrangian geography setup
proof_stages.summary: The smooth question is reformulated for Legendrian submanifolds and exact Lagrangian fillings or cobordisms in symplectizations, with the key comparison being whether Lagrangian geography is more rigid than smooth geography.

proof_stages.label: Generating-family invariants
proof_stages.summary: A Legendrian with a generating family is studied through a difference function whose Morse theory records Reeb chords and yields generating-family cohomology and a duality-symmetric polynomial, while a filling is required to carry a compatible extension of that generating family.

proof_stages.label: Embedded Seidel isomorphism and scarcity
proof_stages.summary: The generating-family version of Seidel’s isomorphism identifies the boundary polynomial with the ordinary cohomology of any embedded compatible filling, forcing polynomials of the form t+2c and determining the filling genus.

proof_stages.label: Immersed Seidel isomorphism and checkmark restrictions
proof_stages.summary: For an immersed compatible filling, a complex combining the topology of the immersion domain with indexed double points is shown to compute the same generating-family cohomology, giving lower bounds on double points, genus/double-point tradeoffs, and mod-two checkmark restrictions.

proof_stages.label: Realization moves for admissible lattice points
proof_stages.summary: The speaker then presents GF-compatible construction moves—Legendrian isotopy, pinch and filling moves, immersed unclasping, and operations adding two double points or adding genus together with one double point—to realize and propagate points in the allowed geography lattice.

proof_stages.label: Computed geographies and further obstructions
proof_stages.summary: The obstruction and realization results are applied to the m(5_2) and 7_4 test cases to display Lagrangian geographies distinct from the smooth ones, after which the talk turns to open questions about stronger A-infinity/product obstructions, action-size refinements, mutation trades, caps, and counting fillings.
